question: A word processor can be used to
solution: word processor (WP) is a device or computer program that provides for input, editing, formatting, and output of text, often with some additional features. ... The functions of a word processor program fall somewhere between those of a simple text editor and a fully functioned desktop publishing program.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these application packages is mostly used by computer users?
solution: Ms Word is an application package mostly used by computer users.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The function of an assembler is to _________
solution: Assembler convert assembly language to machine language for the computer to operate with

options.a: convert basic language to machine language
options.b: convert high level language to machine language
options.c: convert assembly language to machine language
options.d: convert assembly language into high level language

subject: Computer Studies

question: A computer can't boot if it does not have ____________
solution: The Operating system is responsible for the running of the computer. Hence, in its absence, its impossible for the computer to boot.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Fire fox is an example of __________
solution: Fire fox is an application software, because it is not an operating system, hence it can't be classified as a system software because it doesn't contribute to the running of the computer system.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The Caps key on a keyboard is used to___________
solution: Caps Lock is a key on a computer keyboard that allows users to generate letters in uppercase once activated, as in "SAMPLE," without holding down the Shift key. It is a toggle key and can be found on the left side of a computer keyboard below the Tab key.

options.c: Switch between upper and lower case letters

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which key deletes text?
solution: The backspace key is used to delete text, alphabets, etc.
subject: Computer Studies

question: From where can the name of a user account be changed?
solution: Control panel. It is a component of microsoft Windows that provides the ability to view and change system settings. It consists of a set of applets that include adding or removing hardware and software, controlling user accounts, changing accessibility options and accessing networking settings.
subject: Computer Studies

question: For what does WAN stand?
solution: In its simplest form, a wide-area network (WAN) is a collection of local-area networks (LANs) or other networks that communicate with one another. A WAN is essentially a network of networks, with the Internet the world's largest WAN
subject: Computer Studies

question: The acronym IP address has its full meaning to be _________
solution: Internet protocol is the full meaning of the acronym IP.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which doesn't belong to the group?
solution: External memory doesn't belong to the group because it is a type of memory that can be detached from the computer, unlike the other three options that can't be detached from the computer system because they are inbuilt.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Storage which retains data after power cut off is called.
solution: Non volatile storage retains data after power is off because, Non volatile storage is a broad collection of technologies and devices that do not require a continuous power supply to retain data or program code persistently on a short or long term basis.
subject: Computer Studies

question: An inventory management program can assist with ________
solution: All of the above.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In any software package, which of the following versions represents a major improvement on the previous version?
solution:
Windows 2.0 allowed application windows to overlap each other, unlike its predecessor Windows 1.0, which could display only tiled windows. Windows 2.0 also introduced more sophisticated keyboard-shortcuts and the terminology of "Minimize" and "Maximize", as opposed to "Iconize" and "Zoom" in Windows 1.0. It was also the last version of Windows that did not require a hard disk.

With the improved speed, reliability and usability, computers now started becoming a part of daily life for some workers. Desktop icons and use of keyboard shortcuts helped to speed up work
subject: Computer Studies

question: Encryption helps with what?
solution: Encryption deals with the safe transfer of data of information from its source to its destinations, without a third party having access to the information or data being transferred.

options.c: Safe delivery of messages to destination without being accessed by a third party
options.d: interpretation of message and digital data

subject: Computer Studies

question: CRACKING comes under ___________
solution: Computer crime. It is the use of a computer as an instrument to further illegal ends. Such as committing fraud, trafficking in child pornography, cracking etc.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In the files, if order of operation on two or more files are similar, then operation will be
solution: If the order of operation on two or more files is similar, then the operation will be sequential. Sequential operation means that the files will be processed one after the other in the same order as specified.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is a computer file.
solution: A computer file is a computer resource for recording data in a computer storage device, primarily identified by its file name

options.b: A document like bunch of text
options.c: A place on a computer where information is stored in a package
options.d: A physical paper document that entails how a computer works

question: When does the output of a NAND gate =1?
solution: Whenever a 0 is present at an input, NAND gate = 1.

options.a: whenever a 0 is present at an input
options.c: whenever 1 is present at an input

solution: This is what a XOR gate can do.

question: The end users are able to operate at the _______ tier.
solution: The presentation tier is the user interface and communication layer of the application where the end user interacts with the application.

question: A tier in which the database resides along with the query processing language is called
solution: Database (Data) Tier − The database resides along with its query processing languages at this tier. We also have the relations that define the data and their constraints at this level.

question: Java is a ______ language
solution: Java is a high level language because, the program is designed to interact with humans, and not the computer.

question: How many different values can a bit have?
solution: A bit can have 2 different values.

solution: Binary consists of two digits, hence its radix being 2.

question: Which of the following is termed as the minimum error code?
solution: Gray code is considered minimum error code because they are less error prone for mechanical devices that involve making and breaking electrical circuits because they only change in one bit position at a time.

question: The set of rules that defines the combination of symbols that are considered to be correctly structured statements or expressions in that language is called _________
solution: In computer science, the syntax of a computer language is the set of rules that defines the combinations of symbols that are considered to be correctly structured statements or expressions in that language.

question: What controls the way in which the computer system function and provides a means by which users can interact with the computer.
solution: An Operating System (OS) is a software that acts as an interface between computer hardware components and the user.

question: A light sensitive device that converts drawing, printed text or other images into digital form is
solution: A scanner converts hard copy of data and other information to soft copy, by serving as an input device that supplies the computer the information or data contained in the hard copy.

solution: Printer is an output device that is primarily used to provide hardcopy in the form of paper.

question: The joystick typically have a button on _________ used to select the option pointed by the cursor.
solution: The bottom is placed on top of the joystick.

question: Which of the following is a computer not used for?
solution: Computer is used in almost every aspect. Hence, it is almost impossible to say there is an aspect where computer isn't used.

question: The combination of the arithmetic and logic units, as well as the ______________ unit makes up the central processing unit.
solution: The control unit manages and coordinates the operation of a computer system. The ALU is responsible for performing all the arithmetic and bitwise operations. Therefore, both these units combine to form the brain of the computer, ie CPU.
subject: Computer Studies

question: How are the stages of SDLC implemented?
solution: the stages of SDLC are executed sequentially in order to ensure it goes accordingly. Also, they must be done sequential to gets results from previous stages to know what to improve in later stages. Also it does not overlap because, a stage must be concluded before another proceeds.

question: __________________ is the conversion of computer data from one format to another.
solution: Data conversion is the process of translating data from one format to another

question: The delete permission allows one to ____________
solution:
Delete or remove files.
You can delete a permission that is associated with a user account. When you delete a permission, you remove it from use for all user accounts.

options.b: delete or remove files and directories

question: Which of the following is not a model used in problem solving?
solution: Waterfall is not a model used in solving problems.

question: The explorer bar is divided into _________ categories.
solution: The explore bar is divided into three categories.

question: System software has which major function?
solution:
The system software controls the computer system.
System software controls a computer's internal functioning, chiefly through an operating system, and also controls such peripherals as monitors, printers, and storage devices.

question: Which among the following generation computers had expensive operation cost?
solution: The first generation computers had extensive operation cost because of many reasons such as the excess consumption of electricity, high generation of heat, as well as the cost of maintaining it.

solution: Operating system being responsible for the running of the computer system, tells the computer how to use its component, and the most suitable component to use.

question: What does CO stand for in COBOL?
solution: COBOL stands for common business-oriented language. Therefore, CO stands for common

question: What does a linker program do?
solution: from the name linker program, a linker program links a program with other programs needed for its execution. It does this by taking one or more objects files and combining them into a single executable file, library file, or another object file.

options.a: it places the program in the memory for execution
options.b: relocates the program to execute from the specific memory area allocated to it
options.c: Links the program with other programs needed for its execution
options.d: interface the program with the entities generating its input data

subject: Computer Studies

question: In which situation does running application remain active?
solution: Running applications remain active when user is switched because, when user is switched, cache memory is not cleared and the computer system does not go off. Therefore, running apps remain active when user is switched.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Internet explorer is used for
solution: The Internet explorer is used view Web pages. Internet Explorer is a free web browser, commonly called IE or MSIE, that allows users to view web pages on the internet. It is also used to access online banking, online marketing over the internet, listen to and watch streaming videos, and many more. It was introduced by Microsoft in 1995.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does LAN stand for?
solution:
A local area network (LAN) is a collection of devices connected together in one physical location, such as a building, office, or home. A LAN can be small or large, ranging from a home network with one user to an enterprise network with thousands of users and devices in an office or school.

Regardless of size, a LAN's single defining characteristic is that it connects devices that are in a single, limited area.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The Internet is an example of which computer network?
solution: Mesh network topologies are typical of the internet, and certain wide area networks (WANs)
subject: Computer Studies

question: A permanent memory, which has data and instruction to start up the computer and does not erase data after power off.
solution: The read only memory does not erase data after power off because, it is a non volatile memory.

question: A display listing of program options which users can select is called?
solution: The menu consists of various programs users use.

question: The illegal access to a network or computer system is___________
solution: Cracking is trying to get into computer systems in order to steal, corrupt or illegitimately view data.

solution: The cloud access enables one access files from a cloud database than where the files are stored because, anything uploaded to a cloud can be accessed from another computer.

question: The windows explorer is divided into _________ categories.

subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the primary purpose of Microsoft Excel?

solution: This is the quickest way to rename a sheet. Double left click on the sheet tab which you would like to rename.

options.d: Going to File, and clicking Save As

subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the shortcut key to "undo" last action on a Microsoft word page?
solution: To undo an action press Ctrl+Z. If you prefer your mouse, click Undo on the Quick Access Toolbar. You can press Undo (or CTRL+Z) repeatedly if you want to undo multiple steps
subject: Computer Studies

question: ________ is the process of dividing the disk into tracks and sectors
solution: The correct answer is Formatting. The process of dividing the disk into tracks and sectors is called formatting. In computer disk storage, a sector is a subdivision of a track on a magnetic disk or optical disc. When a disk undergoes a low-level format, it is divided into tracks and sectors.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The process of feeding instructions into the computer system is called
solution: In computer science, the general meaning of input is to provide or give something to the computer, in other words, when a computer or device is receiving a command or signal from outer sources, the event is referred to as input to the device
subject: Computer Studies

question: To determine if a computer has the ability to think, a test was conducted named as
solution: The Turing test, originally called the imitation game by Alan Turing in 1950, is a test of a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behaviour equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human. ... If the evaluator cannot reliably tell the machine from the human, the machine is said to have passed the test.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which is not a network for distribution of information?
solution: Ms-word is not a network for distribution of information.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the full form of TCP?
solution: TCP means Transmission Control Protocol.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the full form of UDP?
solution: User datagram protocol (UDP) operates on top of the Internet Protocol (IP) to transmit datagrams over a network.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does RAM and ROM have in common?
solution: RAM and ROM are types of computer memory.

options.b: They were both invented by Bill Gates
options.c: They do not have anything in common

subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the permanent memory built into your computer called?
solution: ROM is an acronym for Read-Only Memory. It refers to computer memory chips containing permanent or semi-permanent data. Unlike RAM, ROM is non-volatile; even after you turn off your computer, the contents of ROM will remain. Almost every computer comes with a small amount of ROM containing the boot firmware.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does an Operating System do?
solution: Operating System (OS) is a program that manages a computer's resources, especially the allocation of those resources among other programs. Typical resources include the central processing unit (CPU), computer memory, file storage, input/output (I/O) devices, and network connections.
subject: Computer Studies

question: An Operating System is a type of?
solution: An operating system (OS) is a system software that manages computer hardware, software resources, and provides common services for computer programs
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the etiquette one should follow while working online?
solution: Netiquette, which is a portmanteau of "net" and "etiquette," refers to using courtesy and politeness when communicating with others online.

options.a: Anything goes . . . providing he/she remains anonymous

subject: Computer Studies

question: ___________ is when someone steals another persons identity.
solution: Identity theft occurs when someone uses another person's personal identifying information, like their name, identifying number, or credit card number, without their permission, to commit fraud or other crimes. The term identity theft was coined in 1964
subject: Computer Studies

question: Destruction of files for malicious reasons such as access by fraudulent people is classified as being
solution: Access to an information system or network that violates the owner or operator's stated security policy is considered unauthorized access.
subject: Computer Studies

question: When an input signal 1 is applied to a NOT gate, the output is ..................
solution: In a NOT gate, no output (0) is there when an input (1) is there.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In Boolean algebra, the bar sign (-) indicates ....................
solution: In modern Boolean algebra, we use the plus (+) symbol to mean OR, the dot (•) symbol to mean AND, and a bar above a variable to mean NOT.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In SQL, which command is used to remove a stored function from the database?
solution: DROP FUNCTION removes the definition of an existing function. To execute this command the user must be the owner of the function. The argument types to the function must be specified, since several different functions can exist with the same name and different argument lists.
subject: Computer Studies

question: .......... provides total solutions to reduce data redundancy, inconsistency, dependence and unauthorized access of data
solution: The Database Management System or DBMS helps to tackle issues as these.

question: Programming language COBOL works best for use in
solution: COBOL, in full Common Business-Oriented Language, high-level computer programming language, one of the first widely used languages and for many years the most popular language in the business community.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Hexadecimal numbers are a mixture of
solution: The hexadecimal number system is described as a 16 digit number representation of numbers from 0 - 9 (decimal digits) and digits from A - F (letters).
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is considered the brain of the computer?
solution:
The CPU is the brain of a computer, containing all the circuitry needed to process input, store data, and output results.
The CPU is constantly following instructions of computer programs that tell it which data to process and how to process it. Without a CPU, we could not run programs on a computer.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The actual physical network of wires, cables and satellite that connects computers and resources throughout the world is the _______________
solution: The Internet (or internet)is the global system of interconnected computer networks that uses the Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP) to communicate between networks and devices.

question: CPU stands for ________________.
solution: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

solution: The computer monitor is a visual display Unit (VDU).A VDU displays images generated by a computer or other electronic device

solution: both A and B

question: Typically, an ATM is used for
solution: An Automated Teller Machine, better known as an ATM, is a specialized computer that makes it convenient for bank account holders to manage their money. It allows them to check their account balances, withdraw or deposit money, transfer money from one account to another, print a statement of account transactions, and even purchase stamp
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the full form of CU?
solution:
CU stands for control unit.
The control unit (CU) is a component of a computer's central processing unit (CPU) that directs the operation of the processor. A CU typically uses a binary decoder to convert coded instructions into timing and control signals that direct the operation of the other units (memory, arithmetic logic unit and input and output devices, etc.).
subject: Computer Studies

question: The rules that govern the structure of a command or programming language is called
solution: Syntax refers to the rules that define the structure of a language. Syntax in computer programming means the rules that control the structure of the symbols, punctuation, and words of a programming language.

question: In general, a file is basically a collection of all related______.
solution: A file is a collection of related data or program records stored on some input/output or auxiliary storage medium.

question: The memory unit receives data and information from which unit?
solution: From the chart or table of data flow, the data moves from the input unit to the control unit where it is determined if it needs a logical or arithmetic function. From the control unit, it is passed on to the arithmetic and logical unit, where it is then processed before being transferred to the memory unit, to be stored till when needed.

DBMS.

The types of system software include: 1.Operating systems or system control program.

question: The number of input combination for a 4 input gate is ______

16 possible combinations

question: When does the output of a NOR gate =0?
solution: The NOR gate is a digital logic gate that implements logical NOR - it behaves according to the truth table to the right. A HIGH output (1) results if both the inputs to the gate are LOW (0); if one or both input is HIGH (1), a LOW output (0) results.
subject: Computer Studies

question: A collection of facts and figures is known to be _________
solution: Data are a set of values of qualitative or quantitative variables about one or more persons or objects, while a datum (singular of data) is a single value of a single variable. They are individual facts, statistics, or items of information, often numeric.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The logical structure of the database can be depicted through its ______
solution: Data model. It is the logical inter relationships and data flow between different data elements involved in the information word. Through the flow chart, the logical structure of a database can be depicted.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The java interpreter translates a java program from ____________ to machine language.
solution: The java interpreter translates a java program from ____________Byte code. This is a program code that has been compiled from source code into low level code designed for a software interpreter. to machine language.
subject: Computer Studies

question: ___________________ is a program meant to be sent from one computer to another across the world wide Web.
solution: applet Is a "small Java" program that can be used in an internetworking environment. These programs can be transferred over the internet from one computer to another and can be displayed on various web browsers.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The hexadecimal digits are 0-9 and A-_____
solution: Hexadecimal has 16 digits ranging from 0 to 9, and A to F.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Binary coded decimals (BCD) numbers express each digit as a ___________
solution: Nibbles are used to express binary coded number's digit
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these is not a programming language
solution: Ios is an operating system not a programming language.
subject: Computer Studies

question: ___________ booting is done by turning on the computer at first instance.
solution: Cold booting is the process where a computer is turned on from a totally powerless state.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What differs a system software from application software?
solution: A system software differs from an application software in so many ways. Of which one is the ability of system software to control and run the computer, while applications software are installed by users to perform certain functions.

options.b: being minimal in terms of space
options.d: unique programs installed by the creators to help run the computer

subject: Computer Studies

question: ___________ is the combination of already processed _____________
solution: Information is the combination of already processed data.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the major purpose of application software?
solution: The major function performed by all application software is to allow the user utilize it in a particular area. Be it security, research, gaming, etc.

options.a: oversee the running of the computer

subject: Computer Studies

question: What kind of data is processed by an analog computer?
solution: An analog computer is the one which is used to process continuously varying data. This changeable yet continuous data is generally referred to as the analog data
subject: Computer Studies

question: Computers that are portable and convenient for users who travel are known as _________________
solution: A laptop computer is a personal computer for mobile use. It has most of the same components as a desktop computer, including a display, a keyboard, a pointing device such as a touchpad (also known as a trackpad) and/or a pointing stick, and speakers into a single unit. A laptop is powered by mains electricity via an AC adapter, and can be used away from an outlet using a rechargeable battery. Laptops are also sometimes called notebook computers, notebooks, ultrabooks or netbooks. This could also be referred to as portable computers as they are easy to carry around.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does ICT stand for?
solution: ICT means Information and Communications Technology
subject: Computer Studies

question: _________ are programmes that affect the effectiveness of a computer system.
solution: Virus is a piece of code which is capable of copying itself and typically has a detrimental effect, such as corrupting the system or destroying data.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these is not a job of the operating system
solution: The operating system doesn't handle HTTP requests for Web pages but a web server does.

options.a: Stopping unauthorised users accessing files that do not belong to them
options.b: Handling HTTP requests for Web pages
options.c: Allocating memory to programs that are running on the computer

subject: Computer Studies

question: _______ is the process of finding errors and fixing them within a program.
solution: Debugging is the process of detecting and removing of existing and potential errors (also called as 'bugs') in a software code that can cause it to behave unexpectedly or crash. To prevent incorrect operation of a software or system, debugging is used to find and resolve bugs or defects
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is not a type of computer on the basis of operation?
solution: Every other option but option A, is a type of computer based on the basis of operation
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is used for modulation and demodulation ?
solution: A modem(modulator-demodulator) is a hardware device that connects a computer or router to a broadband network.
subject: Computer Studies

question: When records are given a new value, it is called
solution: When records are given a new value, it is called 'updation'. This is a process in database management where an existing record is modified with new data. 'Insertion' is adding a new record, 'deletion' is removing a record, and 'retrieval' is fetching data from the database.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The type of computers that are designed to perform complex calculations extremely rapidly are called ?
solution: Supercomputers are designed to perform complex calculations extremely rapidly. They are the fastest and most powerful type of computer, often used for large-scale simulations, such as weather forecasting, climate research, molecular modeling, and physical simulations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: A set of moral principles that regulate the use of computers is called
solution: Computer ethics is the term used to describe a set of moral principles that regulate the use of computers. It involves questions related to privacy, accuracy, property, and access.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The logic gate in the figure above is
solution:
The figure above is the "XOR (exclusive-OR) gate" acts in the same way as the logical "either/or." The output is true if either, but not both, of the inputs are true. The output is false if both inputs are "false" or if both inputs are true. Similarly, the output is 1 if the inputs are different but 0 if the inputs are the same. OR  It's an XOR gate
denoted by Z = A ⊕ B.
subject: Computer Studies

question: A computer code that spreads without the user interaction and can cause network performance problem is
solution: A computer worm is a computer code that spreads without user interaction, they are not always destructive but can cause network performance and stability problems.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The following are input devices except.
solution: The monitor is an output device, not an input device. Input devices are used to input data into a computer system for processing. Examples include a keyboard, mouse, joystick, etc. On the other hand, output devices are used to display or output the processed data. Examples include a monitor, printer, etc.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What category of application package does microsoft excel belong to ?
solution: Microsoft Excel is a spreadsheet package. It is used for storing, organizing, and manipulating data. It provides tools for data visualization such as charts and graphs. Word processing packages like Microsoft Word are used for creating, editing, and formatting text documents. Database management packages are used for storing, retrieving, and managing data in a database. Graphics packages are used for creating and editing images, drawings, and illustrations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: A type of application software that combines the abilities of several general purpose applications in one program is ?
solution: Integrated Packages is an all-in-one application package that includes word processing, spreadsheet, database manager, graphics and communications.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the function of the escape key ?
solution: Escape key can perform various functions like interrupting or cancelling the current process or running program or close a pop-up window.

options.c: to interrupt or cancel the current process or running program

subject: Computer Studies

question: A set of programs that direct computers to perform specific information processing activities for end users is
solution: Application software is the set of programs necessary to carry out operations for a specified application.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What protocol is used between Email servers?
solution: The Simple Mail Transfer Protocol is an internet standard communication protocol for electronic mail transmission.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The following are the methods of file security except
solution:
The methods of file security are
- Use of back-ups
- Use of anti-virus
- Password
- Proper label of storage devices.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The first step in a CPU instruction cycle is
solution: Fetch is the first step of CPU instruction cycle also called fetch-and-execute cycle, fetch-decode-execute cycle, or FDX.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is not an example of graphics packages
solution: Quattro Pro is not a graphics package. It is a spreadsheet program developed by Borland and now managed by Corel. It provides tools for data management and analysis. Havard Graphics, Freelance, and Corel Draw are examples of graphics packages. They provide tools for creating and editing images, drawings, and illustrations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The process of finding and correcting errors in the program code is called ?
solution: Debugging is the process of finding and correcting errors in the program code. It involves identifying, isolating, and fixing the problems or bugs in computer program code. The other options do not accurately describe this process. Editing is the process of making changes to the code, but it does not necessarily involve finding and correcting errors. Coding is the process of writing the code, and testing is the process of checking the code to see if it works as expected.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following components of the computer is referred to as the administrative section ?
solution: CPU is referred to as the administrative section of the computer, it is also called the brain of the computer.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is not a third generation language?
solution: Assembly language is a low-level programming language that is specific to a particular computer architecture. It is more difficult to learn and use than higher-level programming languages, but it offers more control over the hardware.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these commands will reboot the computer ?
solution: The command 'Ctrl + Alt + Del' is a common PC shortcut that restarts the computer. It is also known as 'Three-finger salute'. This command is used to bring up the Task Manager or the Windows Security options (including the option to restart or shut down) in Windows operating systems.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The type of database in which the data are connected in different files by using common data elements or a key field is ?
solution: Relational database connects data in different files by using a common data elements or a key field.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Anti-virus software is an example of ?
solution: software utilities is a type of computer system software also referred to as service program, tool, service routine, or utility routine, they help to analyze, configure, optimize or maintain a computer.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the shortcut for creating a new document ?
solution: The shortcut for creating a new document is Ctrl + N. This is a standard shortcut across many applications and operating systems.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these criteria is not important while classifying files ?
solution: While classifying files, the storage medium is not a criterion. The classification of files is based on the nature of the content in the file, the organization method, and the size of the file. The storage medium is where the file is stored, which does not affect the classification of the file.

options.b: Nature of content in the file

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is a valid variable name in Python?
solution: Variable names in Python must start with a letter or underscore, and can only contain letters, numbers, and underscores. The other options are invalid because they either start with a number (option C) or contain a special character (option D).
subject: Computer Studies

question: How many bits are in one nibble ?
solution: A nibble is a four-bit aggregation, or half an octet. It is also known as half-byte or tetrade. In a networking or telecommunication context, the nibble is often called a semi-octet, quadbit, or quartet. A nibble has sixteen (2^4) possible values.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The only possible number base for 235\(_x\) among the following is ?
solution:
A number cannot contain digits that are greater than its base.
therefore 235 can possible be in base 6.
subject: Computer Studies

question: At what stage in system development life cycle are all data documented in the form of detailed data flow diagrams(DFDs)
solution: During the System Analysis stage of the System Development Life Cycle (SDLC), all data are documented in the form of detailed Data Flow Diagrams (DFDs). The System Analysis stage involves understanding and documenting the business processes and requirements of the new system.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Translators are type of ?
solution: Translators are a type of system software. They are used to convert source code written in one programming language (the source language) into another programming language (the target language). Examples of translators include compilers, interpreters, and assemblers. System software is a type of computer software designed to run a computer's hardware and application programs. It includes the operating system, device drivers, diagnostic tools, servers, windowing systems, utilities, and more.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the difference between an assembler and a compiler ?
solution: An assembler converts assembly level language code into machine language code while a compiler converts high level programming language code to a machine language code.

options.a: an assembler converts a machine language code into assembly level language code while a compiler converts high level programming language to a machine language code.
options.b: an assembler converts assembly level language code into machine language code while a compiler converts high level programming language code to a machine language code.
options.c: a compiler converts assembly level language code into machine lange while an assembler converts high level programming language to a machine language code.
options.d: they both convert assembly level language code into machine lange

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these storage devices can act as a buffer between the CPU and the main memory ?
solution: Cache memory is a very high speed semiconductor memory which can speed up CPU, it acts as a buffer between the CPU and main memory.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Evaluate \(1011_2 + 122_4\), leaving your answer in base 10.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What part of the central processing unit coordinates other units and manages the computer resources ?
solution: The Control Unit of the Central Processing Unit (CPU) coordinates other units and manages the computer resources. It directs the operation of the processor, and interacts with the other hardware components in the system to carry out the instructions of a program. It is responsible for fetching, decoding, and executing instructions from the memory unit and performing arithmetic and logical operations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does R stands for in the CRUD acronym ?
solution: In the CRUD acronym, R stands for 'Read'. CRUD stands for Create, Read, Update, and Delete. These are the four basic functions of persistent storage in databases.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these is not true about peer-to-peer network ?
solution: A peer-to-peer (P2P) network is a decentralized network where each computer (peer) acts as both a client and a server. This means that there are no dedicated servers and no hierarchy among the computers. However, P2P networks are generally not known for having a strong security system. This is because each peer has equal access to the resources, which can lead to potential security risks.

options.b: it has a strong security system
options.c: all computers in this network are known as peers
options.d: there is no hierarchy among the computers

subject: Computer Studies

question: The Output will be a HIGH for any case when one or more inputs are one for a(an)
solution: An OR gate is a digital logic gate that implements logical disjunction - it behaves according to the truth table to the right. A HIGH output (1) results if one or more of the inputs to the gate is HIGH (1). If neither input is high, a LOW output (0) results. In another sense, the function of OR effectively finds the maximum between two binary digits, just as the complementary AND function finds the minimum.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What type of booting does the computer go through when starting up from a powered down ?
solution: Cold booting is when you start a computer from a powered-down, or off, state. This is often referred to as a system restart or a system reset. Warm booting, on the other hand, is restarting the computer while it's powered on. Rebooting and soft booting are general terms for restarting a computer and can refer to either cold or warm booting.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What type of booting does the computer go through when starting up from a powered down ?
solution: Cold booting is when you start a computer from a powered-down, or off, state. This is often referred to as a system restart or a system reset. Warm booting, on the other hand, is restarting the computer while it's powered on. Rebooting and soft booting are general terms for restarting a computer and can refer to either cold or warm booting.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The connection between two or more computers to share resources or allow electronic communication is called ?
solution: A network consists of two or more computers that are linked together in order to share resources, exchange files or allow electronic communication
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is the standard keyboard layout ?
solution: The standard keyboard layout is QWERTY. This layout was patented by Christopher Sholes who invented the typewriter and later adapted to the world's first computers.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Analog, digital and hybrid computers are classification of computers based on
solution: Computers are classified into analog, digital, and hybrid based on the type of data-handling techniques they use. Analog computers work with continuous data and are used for tasks such as measuring and controlling voltages. Digital computers work with discrete data and are used for tasks such as word processing and spreadsheet calculations. Hybrid computers combine the features of both analog and digital computers.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is not an application software
solution: Linux is not an application software. It is an example of system software.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is NOT a high-level programming language?
solution: Assembly language is a low-level programming language that is specific to a particular computer architecture. It is more difficult to learn and use than high-level programming languages, but it offers more control over the hardware.
subject: Computer Studies

question: what is the first computing machine invented ?
solution: The Abacus was invented between 2700 and 2300 BC, it is the first computing machine known to mankind.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The default file extension for PowerPoint version 2007 and newer is ?
solution: The default file extension for PowerPoint version 2007 and newer is .pptx. This extension stands for PowerPoint Open XML, which is a file format used by PowerPoint 2007 and newer for saving presentations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following can be used to select the entire document ?
solution: The keyboard shortcut 'Ctrl + A' is used to select the entire document in most word processing software. This command selects all text or other content in the document.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following does not replicate itself in an infected computer?
solution: trojan horse is a malicious software program that hides inside other programs, trojan horse does not usually spread by themselves.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The last cycle of data processing where data and information are preserved for future is called
solution: The last cycle of data processing where data and information are preserved for future use is called 'storage'. This is the phase where data or information is saved either temporarily or permanently for future use. The 'output' is the result of data processing but not necessarily preserved for future use. 'Processing' is the action of manipulating data. 'Record' is a piece of stored information.
subject: Computer Studies

question: A device that sends and receives printed pages or images over telephone lines by digitizing the material with an internal optical scanner and transmitting the information as electronic signals is a
solution: Fax machine is a device that sends and receives printed pages or images over telephone lines by digitizing the material with an internal optical scanner and transmitting the information as electronic signals.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The first computer made available for commercial use was
solution: On June 14, 1951, the U.S. Census bureau dedicates UNIVAC(Universal Automatic Computer), the first commercially produced electronic digital computer the US.
subject: Computer Studies

question: An action performed in the GUI operating systems to hide a window but keep the program running in the background is ?
solution: Minimize is an action performed in the GUI operating systems to hide a window but keep the program running in the background
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the shortcut for performing warm booting ?
solution: Warm booting refers to the process of restarting a computer system which is already on. This can be done by pressing the Ctrl + Alt + Del keys simultaneously. This is a common shortcut for warm booting in many operating systems.

options.a: y pressing the Ctrl + Alt keys simultaneously
options.b: y pressing the Ctrl + Alt + F1 keys simultaneously
options.c: y pressing the Ctrl + Del keys simultaneously
options.d: by pressing the Ctrl + Alt + Del keys simultaneously

subject: Computer Studies

question: EDVAC was produced using which of these concepts ?
solution: EDVAC (Electronic Discrete Variable Automatic Computer) was produced using the 'stored program' concept. This concept is a design architecture for computers where the instructions to be executed by the computer are stored in the same memory that holds the data. 'Circuit switching', 'packet routing', and 'packet switching' are concepts related to network communication, not computer design.
subject: Computer Studies

question: When the computer reboots itself without triggering the hardware based reset, it is called ?
solution: soft or warm reboot involves restarting a computer under software control without triggering the hardware based reset.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Who invented the Napier Bones
solution: Napier's bones is a manually-operated calculating device created by John Napier of Merchiston for calculation of products and quotients of numbers. The method was based on the ancient mathematical principle known as the Arabic lattice, or gelosia multiplication.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the difference between internal and external modem
solution: Internal modems are PCI or ISA expansion cards, they tend to be simple to install while external modems are traditionally plug into a serial port on the computer.

options.a: external modems are PCI or ISA expansion cards while internal modems are traditionally plug into a serial port on the computer.
options.b: internal modems are PCI or ISA expansion cards while external modems are traditionally plug into a serial port on the computer.
options.c: external modems are PCI or ISA expansion cards while internal modems are USB modems
options.d: they are PCI or ISA expansion cards

subject: Computer Studies

question: The two types of operating system are
solution: Operating systems can be categorized into two types based on their user interface: Command Line Interface (CLI) and Graphical User Interface (GUI). CLI requires users to type commands to interact with the system, while GUI uses visual elements like icons, windows, and buttons for interaction.

options.a: command line and graphical user interface

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these application packages can be used to create a spreadsheet ?
solution: Microsoft Excel is an application package that is specifically designed for creating spreadsheets. It provides features for creating, editing, and managing data in a tabular format.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In technical feasibility, the priority is ?
solution: In technical feasibility, the priority is to check whether the problem can be solved using existing technology and resources available.

options.a: whether the probolem can be solved in the user's environment
options.b: if the likely benefits outweight the cost of solving the problem
options.c: whether the problem can be solved using existing technology and resources available
options.d: whether the problem can be solved without causing any social issues

subject: Computer Studies

question: What generation of computer is characterized by the use of vacuum tubes for circuitry and magnetic drums for main memory?
solution: The first generation computer systems used vacuum tubes for circuitry and magnetic drums for main memory, and they were often enormous, taking up entire rooms.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Large computers are classified as
solution: mainframe computers are large and powerful computers that handle the processing for many users simultaneously
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following allows devices on one network to communicate with devices on another network ?
solution: A computer that sits between different networks or applications. The gateway converts information, data or other communications from one protocol or format to another.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The diagrammatic representation of an algorithm is
solution: The diagrammatic representation of an algorithm is a flowchart. A flowchart is a type of diagram that represents an algorithm, workflow or process, showing the steps as boxes of various kinds, and their order by connecting them with arrows.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The process where a programmer converts a program specifications into computer instructions is called ?
solution: The process where a programmer converts a program specifications into computer instructions is called Coding. Testing is the process of checking the functionality of the program, System design is the process of defining the architecture, components, modules, interfaces, and data for a system to satisfy specified requirements, and Debugging is the process of finding and resolving defects or problems within a computer program.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of these programming languages is the most suitable for a business data processing?
solution: COBOL (Common Business-Oriented Language) is the most suitable for business data processing. It was specifically designed for business data processing needs such as arithmetic operations, file handling, and record management. BASIC and FORTRAN are more suited for educational and scientific computations respectively, while ALGOL is more for algorithmic representation.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The term used to describe when new information replaces old information or data is
solution: In computing, when new information replaces old information or data, it is referred to as 'overwrite'. This is because the new data is written over the old data, effectively replacing it. Therefore, the correct answer is 'overwrite'.
subject: Computer Studies

question: ___ is the type of computer that is designed to operate on two states, 0 and 1.
solution: A digital computer is a type of computer that operates on digital data, which is represented in binary form (0 and 1). These computers perform calculations using binary digits and can process both numerical and non-numerical data.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is an example of graphics software ?
solution: Adobe Illustrator is a vector graphics editor and design program developed and marketed by Adobe Inc. It is an example of graphics software, which is used to create, edit, and manipulate visual images.
subject: Computer Studies

question: which communication channel allows the sending of information in one direction only?
solution: simplex mode allows the sending of information in one direction only, which is similar to a one-way street.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following monitors has only two colours
solution: A monochrome monitor is a type of CRT computer monitor which was very common in the early days of computing, from the 1960s through the 1980s, before color monitors became popular. They are designed to display one color of information (usually green, amber, or white) along with a different color background (usually black). Therefore, the correct answer is 'monochrome monitor'.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Computers manipulate data in many ways, and this manipulation is called ?
solution: Computers manipulate data in many ways, and this manipulation is called processing. Data processing refers to the conversion of raw data into meaningful output by performing computations, logical comparisons, and other operations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The computer that combines the functions of both analog and digital computers is called ?
solution: A hybrid computer is a type of computer that combines the characteristics of both digital and analog computers. It is designed to offer the speed of analog equipment and the accuracy and memory of digital equipment.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In BASIC, REM shows a statement to be
solution: REM is an abbreviation for remark and it's the way you add comments to your code in BASIC.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Computers that are small and low cost are referred to as ?
solution: Micro computers are small and low cost digital computers, which usually consists of a micro-processor, a storage unit, an input channel and an output channel.
subject: Computer Studies

question: If the control unit controls other units of the CPU, which unit stores instructions,data and intermediate results.
solution: The memory unit of the CPU stores instructions, data, and intermediate results. The control unit controls the operation of the CPU and hence the computer. The arithmetic section performs arithmetic operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. The logic section performs logical operations like comparison of values.
subject: Computer Studies

question: The closest computer language to human is ?
solution: High level languages give formats close to English language and the purpose of developing high level language is to enable people to write programs easily and in their own native language environment.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Translating the problem statement into a series of sequential steps describing what the program must do is known as
solution: Algorithm design is the process of listing all the instructions which are to be performed by the computer at different stages.
subject: Computer Studies

question: One of the following is not a good way to prevent virus.
solution:
Ways of preventing virus
- use of antivirus software
- use of firewall
- exercising care in giving out personal and vital information
- encryptions

options.c: carelessly exposing your vital and personal information

subject: Computer Studies

question: The first stage of data processing activities is ?
solution: The first stage of data processing is the collection or gathering of data and recording it in a usable form.
subject: Computer Studies

question:
Using Boolean identities, reduce the given Boolean expression:
A(A+1) + A(B+0) + C.1
solution: Using Boolean identities, the given Boolean expression A(A+1) + A(B+0) + C.1 can be reduced as follows: A.1 + A.B + C = A + A.B + C = A + C. The Boolean identity A + A.B = A is used here, which states that if A is true, the whole expression is true regardless of the value of B.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In computer architecture, the type of bus that connects the major components of a computer system is
solution: In computer architecture, the system bus is the main communication route that connects the major components of a computer system, such as the CPU, memory, and input/output devices. It is composed of the data bus, address bus, and control bus. Therefore, the correct answer is 'system bus'.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following controls the way in which the computer system functions and provides a means by which users can interact with the computer.
solution: Operating system controls the way in which the computer system functions and provides a means by which users can interact with the computer.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which type of computer functions as a central repository, providing hardware, software, and other resources to multiple clients connected over a network?
solution:
Servers are specifically designed to act as central repositories on a network. They provide hardware resources (like storage space and processing power), software resources (like applications and databases), and other resources (like network access) to multiple clients (other computers or devices) connected to the network.

Mainframes are used for large-scale data processing workstations are intended for individual use, and supercomputers are designed for highly complex computations.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does the 'Thesaurus' feature in word processing packages help users with?
solution: The Thesaurus feature in word processing packages helps users find synonyms (similar words) and antonyms (opposite words) for words in their document

options.a: Find synonyms and antonyms of words

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which access method is least efficient for data retrieval?
solution:
Sequential access requires data to be accessed in a sequential order from the beginning of the file until the desired data is found. This method is inefficient when the required data is not near the beginning of the file, as it necessitates reading through all preceding data.

In contrast, direct access and indexed enable faster retrieval by allowing immediate access to specific data locations without the need for sequential scanning,Random access also provides efficient access to any data location in the file directly. Therefore, sequential access is the least efficient method for data retrieval among the choice
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which application package is used for developing websites?
solution: Adobe Dreamweaver is a web development application that provides features for coding websites using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. It offers tools for editing code, managing website files, and previewing the website during development.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following best describes software?
solution: Software refers to logical sequences of coded instructions (programs and applications) that tell the computer what task to perform.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following best describes the SDLC?
solution: The SDLC (System Development Life Cycle) is a structured process for planning designing creating deploying and maintaining information systems. It's a framework that ensures a systematic approach to developing high-quality and efficient systems.

options.a: A programming language for system development
options.b: A framework for developing information system

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which key on a keyboard is used to access special functions like volume control or brightness?
solution: Function keys are often marked "Fl " to "F12" and perform specific actions depending on the software or computer.

question: Which stage of the SDLC involves evaluating the system to ensure it meets user needs and making necessary adjustments?
solution:
The review stage, also sometimes called testing involves evaluating the developed system against the initial requirements and user needs. This stage identifies any gaps, errors, or usability issues.

Based on the review findings,adjustments and bug fixes are made to ensure the system meets the intended purpose and provides a satisfactory user
experience.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the relationship between a 'record' and a 'file'?
solution: A file is a collection of multiple records, each containing related fields

options.b: A file is a type of record
options.c:
o
A record and a file are the same

subject: Computer Studies

question: What was the primary advancement of the EDVAC over the ENIAC?
solution:
The key difference between ENIAC and EDVAC lies in how they stored and executed instructions. ENIAC had a fixed program design, meaning the instructions were wired into the machine and could not be changed easily. EDVAC, on the other hand, introduced the concept of a stored-program architecture. Instructions were stored in memory alongside allowing for greater flexibility and easier modification of programs.

This innovation paved the way for modern computers with stored programs on hard drives or other storage devices.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the term for the unauthorized modification of data within a database by exploiting vulnerabilities in an application's software?
solution: SQL injection involves inserting malicious SQL statements into an application's input fields to manipulate the database and gain unauthorized access to data.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Why is it important to be stable, steady, and patient during program development?
solution: Being stable, steady, and patient helps in reducing errors and ensuring the quality of the program.

options.b: To reduce the likelihood of making errors
options.c: To ensure the program is written quickly
options.d: To make the program more complex

subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the purpose of the design phase in program development?
solution: The purpose of the design phase is to create a flowchart and algorithm that outline the logical steps the program will take to solve the problem. This helps in planning the program's structure before coding begins.

options.c: To create a flow chart and algorithm

subject: Computer Studies

question: What type of printer uses toner to print documents?
solution: Laser printers use toner to produce high-quality text and graphics.

solution:
The output of the expression for the circuit is shown above:

A  B+BC
subject: Computer Studies

question: Microprocessors became widely used in which generation of computers?
solution:
The invention and widespread use of microprocessors marked the fourth generation of computers. Microprocessors, essentially complete CPUs on a single chip, led to a dramatic increase in processing power, miniaturization, and affordability of computers.

Microprocessors became the cornerstone of modern computing paving the way for personal computers (PCs) and the continued evolution of computing power we see today.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is a flowchart?
solution: A flowchart is a visual tool that translates the steps of an algorithm into an easy-to-understand diagram. It uses shapes like rectangles, diamonds, and arrows to represent processes, decision points, and the flow of logic within the algorithm.

options.a: A visual representation of an algorithm, using geometric shapes and symbols to depict the flow of logic.
options.b: A collection of data tables used to organize and analyze information.
options.c: A written explanation of the thought process behind solving a problem, without a visual representation.
options.d: A complex code snippet written in a specific programming language.

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which network device provides a central point for connecting devices within a network and manages data traffic between them?
solution: A switch provides a central point for connecting devices within a network and manages data traffic between them at Layer 2 of the OSI model.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is a cloud storage service?
solution: Dropbox is a popular cloud storage service that allows users to store and share files online. OneDrive, Google Drive, and iCloud are other examples of cloud storage services commonly used for this purpose.
subject: Computer Studies

question: In graphic design, what does the term DPI stand for?
solution: DPI stands for Dots Per Inch and is a measure of the resolution of an image, indicating the number of dots of ink or pixels in one inch.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is a common security threat that can compromise your computer files?
solution: Weak passwords are a major security threat. Hackers can use brute-force attacks or password-cracking techniques to guess weak passwords and gain access to your files.

options.b: Weak passwords are used for user accounts and file access.
options.d: Physical damage to your computer hardvvare

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which characteristic of a computer refers to its ability to minimize energy, consumption and resource usage?
solution: Efficiency in computing pertains to the optimal use of resources such as energy, processing power, and memory.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which operation combines multiple files into a single archive file?
solution: Compressing files combines them into a single archive file, such as a ZIP file, reducing storage space and simplifying file management.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does the term "formula" refer to in a spreadsheet?
solution: A formula in a spreadsheet refers to a predefined calculation or operation that performs specific calculations based on cell references and operators.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What are some key components of an ICT system?
solution: ICT systems encompass hardware (physical devices like computers), software (programs and applications), and network (communication channels) that together enable information processing storage and communication.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which toolbar in word processing packages provides options for adjusting paragraph alignment, indentation,and spacing.
solution: The formatting toolbar is specifically designed for controlling the appearance of your paragraphs. This includes options for setting alignment (left, right, center, justified), adjusting indentation (left and right), and modifying spacing settings (before and after paragraphs, line spacing).
subject: Computer Studies

question:
How can you add speaker notes to a PowerPoint slide?
You can add speaker notes by clicking on "View" and then selecting "Notes Page" to open the notes pane where you can
edit your notes.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is a potential drawback of using high-level programming languages?
solution: High-level programming languages provide abstraction from the hardware, which simplifies development but can introduce overhead. This abstraction layer can lead to slower execution speeds compared to low-level programming languages that interact more directly with the hardware.

options.a: High-level languages provide a wider range of built-in functionalities.
options.b: High-level languages are often more efficient than low-level languages.
options.c: High-level languages can sometimes have slower execution speeds due to the abstraction layer.
options.d: High-level languages require a deeper understanding of computer architecture.

subject: Computer Studies

question: What type of ICT is used for immersive gaming solutions?
solution: Virtual Reality (VR) headsets are specifically designed for immersive gaming and simulations, providing users with a virtual environment that they can interact with in a realistic and engaging manner. Augmented Reality (AR) visors overlay digital information onto the real world, while PEDs and 3600 panoramic viewers typically do not offer the same level of immersive experience as VR headsets.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does the acronym ISP stand for?
solution: The acronym ISP stands for Internet Service Provider. An Internet Service Provider (ISP) is a company that provides individuals and organizations access to the Internet and related services such as website building and virtual hosting. ISPs provide the technology and infrastructure needed for users to connect to the Internet, whether through dial-up, DSL, cable, fiber-optic, or wireless connections.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the primary purpose of using a query in MS Access?
solution: Queries are a powerful tool in MS Access for retrieving specific data from your tables. You can define criteria to filter the data based on specific conditions, allowing you to focus on relevant information for analysis or reporting.

options.a: To create a user-friendly interface for entering editing and displaying data in the database.
options.b: To define the overall structure of the tables and their relationships within the database.
options.c:
To retrieve specific data from one or more tables based on defined criteria, allowing for filtering and sorting of
information.
options.d: To visually represent data trends and relationships through charts and graphs.

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which operation combines multiple files into a single archive file?
solution: Compressing files combines them into a single archive file, such as a ZIP file, reducing storage space and simplifying file management.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the advantage of using cell references in formulas compared to entering constant values?
solution: Cell references are crucial for maintaining dynamic formulas. When a value in a referenced cell changes, the formula automatically recalculates the result based on the updated data. This saves time and reduces errors compared to manually entering constant values within the formula itself.

options.a: Cell references make formulas more complex and difficult to understand.
options.b: Cell references are only necessary for complex formulas with multiple functions.
options.c: Cell references are only necessary for complex formulas with multiple functions.
options.d: Cell references improve formula accuracy by reducing manual data entry.

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which type of software acts as an intermediary between hardware and applications?
solution: System software, such as operating systems, acts as an intermediary between hardvvare and application software.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the main advantage of using hexadecimal over binary?
solution: Hexadecimal is easier to lead and write compared to binary because it uses fewer digits to represent the same value.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which type of attack floods a network or server with excessive traffic to make it unavailable to legitimate users?
solution:
A DoS attack floods a network or server with excessive traffic or requests, disrupting its normal operation and making I
unavailable to legitimate users.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the term for an email scam that tries to trick people into giving out personal information?
solution: Phishing is a type of email scam where attackers attempt to trick recipients into providing personal information such as passwords and credit card numbers by pretending to be a legitimate entity.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which type of computer would a small business most likely use for everyday tasks?
solution: Small businesses typically use microcomputers for everyday tasks such as word processing accounting and internet access.
subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of interpreters?
solution: Interpreters translate code line by line, not all at once.

options.a: They are generally slower than compilers.
options.c: They execute code line by line.
options.d: They translate code into machine code at once

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which database organization uses parent-child relationships to organize data in a tree-like structure?
solution: Hierarchical databases organize data in a tree-like structure where each "parent" record can have multiple "child" records. This structure is suitable for situations where data has a clear superior-subordinate relationship, like a file system on a computer where folders can contain subfolders.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the main purpose of device drivers?
solution: Device drivers are specialized system software that allows the operating system to communicate with hardware devices.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What distinguishes open-source software from proprietary software?
solution: Open source software allows users to view, modify, and distribute the source code, while proprietary software restricts access to its source code and distribution.

options.a: Open source software is free, while proprietary software requires purchase.
options.b: Open-source software is more secure than proprietary software.
options.c: Open-source software is more secure than proprietary software.
options.d: Open source software allows modification and redistribution, while proprietary software does not.

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which of the following data types is used to represent textual data in computers?
solution: The string data type is used to represent textual data in computers.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the function of the motherboard in a computer system?
solution: The motherboard is the main circuit board that connects all hardware components of a computer.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What does SSD stand for, and what is its primary advantage?
solution:
Solid State Drive, faster speed.

SSD stands for Solid-State Drive.
The primary advantage of SSDs over traditional hard disk drives (HDDs) is their significantly faster speed. This is due to their lack of moving parts, which eliminates the time it takes for the drive head to seek data on a spinning disk. While SSDs can be more expensive than HDDs, their performance benefits, especially in tasks like booting up, loading applications, and transferring files, make them a popular choice for many users.

options.c: Simple Storage Disk, ease of use

subject: Computer Studies

question: Which statement is used to skip the execution of the current iteration and start the next iteration in a loop?
solution: The continue statement skips the rest of the current loop iteration and continues with the next iteration. It is often used to skip certain values in a loop based on a specific condition.
subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the key innovation of the Analytical Engine?
solution: The Analytical Engine, designed by Charles Babbage, though never fully built, proposed a more general-purpose computing machine with an integrated memory unit to store data and programs, separate from the processing unit. This concept became fundamental in later computers.

options.a: It utilized a steam-powered mechanism for calculations.
options.b: It introduced the concept of a central pmcessing unit (CPU) for calculations.
options.c: It incorporated the idea of a memory unit to store data and programs.
options.d: It employed punch cards for inputting instructions and data.

subject: Computer Studies

question: What is the benefit of using a bulleted or numbered list in your document?
solution: Bulleted and numbered lists are valuable tools for presenting information clearly and organized. They visually separate items, making them easier to read and understand, especially for points within a series or sequence.

options.a: To automatically insert page breaks after each list item, forcing them onto separate pages.
options.b: To automatically change the font style and size of the text within the list.
options.c: To add decorative borders and background colors to specific sections of your document.
options.d: To highlight important points and improve readability by visually separating items.

subject: Computer Studies

question: What are some common criteria used to classify computer files?
solution:
Common criteria for classifying files include:
*File type (document, image, audio, video, etc.)
*Content (project name, subject matter, date, etc.)
*Project or task association
*User (personal, work, client-specific)

options.b: Operating system compatibility and hardware requirements.
options.c: File size and creation date only.
options.d: Font style and color used within the document itself.

This saves time and reduces errors compared to manually entering constant values within the formula itself.

options.a: Cell references improve formula accuracy by reducing manual data entry.

question: When data is organized, processed, and presented in a meaningful context, it becomes:
solution: Information is the result of organizing and processing raw data to provide context, relevance, and meaning. It is essential for decision-making and communication. Metadata refers to data that describes other data, while a fact is a piece of information that is considered to be true. Structured Query Language (SQL) is a programming language used for managing data in relational database management systems, not the transformation of raw.' data into meaningful context.

solution: The '%' operator calculates the modulus or remainder of a division operation in programming.
